title: alchemilla vulgaris subvar. heteropoda
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. heteropoda, also known as the lady's mantle, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family. This perennial herb has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, which make it useful for treating wounds, burns, and digestive issues. The health benefits of lady's mantle include its ability to reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding, soothe skin irritations, and act as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of lady's mantle can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support women's reproductive health and promote overall well-being.
